name: Monte de Boadilla y Parque de las Presillas – circular desde Parque de Polvoranca, Leganés
region_intro: Mountain biking around Leganés offers access to diverse terrain, from local green spaces to the extensive nearby Casa de Campo Park. The region features a network of cycling routes that include both paved and unpaved sections, catering to various skill levels. Leganés itself provides unique natural features like the sand dunes in Dunes Park and tranquil settings in parks such as La Chopera and Parque Polvoranca. This varied landscape provides a range of experiences for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Leganés The most popular mountain bike trail is Monte de Boadilla y Parque de las Presillas – circular desde Parque de Polvoranca, Leganés, a 36.4 miles (58.6 km) moderate trail that takes 4 hours 39 minutes to complete. This route starts from Parque de Polvoranca and explores the Monte de Boadilla area. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Lake of Casa de Campo – Pine Forest of Casa de Campo loop from San Nicasio, a moderate 27.9 miles (45.0 km) path. This loop navigates through the varied tracks of the expansive Casa de Campo Park. Local mountain bikers also love the Las Presillas Park – Descent loop from Leganés, a 28.5 miles (45.9 km) trail leading through Las Presillas Park, often completed in about 3 hours 41 minutes. Mountain biking around Leganés is defined by accessible green spaces, varied terrain including sand dunes, and an extensive cycling network. The routes offer options for different ability levels, from leisurely rides to more demanding mountain biking. The routes in Leganés are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 250 reviews. More than 2500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Leganés's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Leganés?
A: Leganés offers a diverse network of over 25 mountain bike trails, catering to various skill levels. These routes explore local green spaces and provide access to the extensive nearby Casa de Campo Park.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Leganés?
A: The terrain around Leganés is quite varied. You'll find a mix of paved and unpaved sections, including technical dirt paths and gravel. Unique natural features like the sand dunes in Dunes Park also offer distinct landscapes for exploration.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Leganés?
A: Yes, Leganés has several mountain bike trails suitable for beginners. There are 7 easy routes that provide a gentle introduction to mountain biking in the area, often featuring mostly paved surfaces or less challenging unpaved sections.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in Leganés?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Leganés offers 3 difficult mountain bike routes. These trails typically feature more demanding segments, greater elevation gains, and require good fitness and technical ability.

Q: What natural landscapes or viewpoints can I see while mountain biking in Leganés?
A: While mountain biking, you can explore diverse natural settings. The nearby Casa de Campo Park offers extensive woodlands and a large lake, while Leganés itself features unique areas like Dunes Park with its sand dunes. For a specific route with views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31956713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Madrid Skyline – Pine Grove of the Seven Sisters loop from La Serna&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible from mountain bike trails in Leganés?
A: Yes, you can find some charming water features near the trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1516512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Polvoranca Park Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight, and you might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5035208" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quinta de la Fuente del Berro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, offering pleasant spots for a break.

Q: What points of interest are there near the mountain bike trails in Leganés?
A: Beyond the trails, you can explore various points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/664165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa de Campo Lake&lt;/a&gt; is a popular spot, and you might also pass by historical structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1499151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toledo Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. For a cultural experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4613340" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almudena Cathedral&lt;/a&gt; is also within reach.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails in Leganés that are suitable for families or children?
A: Leganés offers several parks like La Chopera and Valdegrullas, which provide tranquil settings and easier paths suitable for family rides. Many of the 7 easy routes are also great options for families looking for less strenuous cycling experiences.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Leganés?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Leganés are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961593817" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte de Boadilla y Parque de las Presillas – circular desde Parque de Polvoranca, Leganés&lt;/a&gt;, which is a popular moderate loop.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Leganés?
A: The mountain biking community highly rates the trails around Leganés, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 250 reviews. Riders often praise the accessible green spaces, the varied terrain, and the extensive cycling network that caters to different ability levels.

Q: Is it possible to reach mountain bike trailheads in Leganés using public transport?
A: Leganés is well-connected, and many local parks and starting points for trails are accessible via public transport. For routes venturing into areas like Casa de Campo, public transport options from Leganés can also get you close to the park's entrances.

Q: Are there places to park near the mountain bike trail starting points in Leganés?
A: Yes, many of the trail starting points, especially those located in or near urban parks like Parque de Polvoranca, offer convenient parking facilities. It's generally easy to find parking close to the beginning of popular routes.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Leganés?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Leganés is typically during spring and autumn when the weather is mild and pleasant. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable, while winters are generally mild but can be damp.
tips: Nice lake very close to the center of Leganés. It is located within the grounds of the Parque de Polvoranca, a nice place to escape from the city where you can enjoy a variety of paths and paths in good condition to alternate your circuits!

A narrow trail that is much more fun going down.
You have to be careful, because at certain times it can be crowded, especially by walkers going in the opposite direction.
Vegetation can play tricks on you in spring and winter. In summer it is advisable to be aware of the sandbanks in certain curves.
It is the most fun trail in Boadilla del Monte.

Las Presillas Park offers a beautiful escape to connect with nature. Very close to the urban center of Alcorcón, inside the park you can find tables and benches, trails of easy difficulty ideal for the whole family to enjoy the bike ride!
